game_description: Resource trading and area control, 3-4 players.
filter_1_label: Do you have the most victory points?

strategies.strategy_detailed: Rate spots by total pips (dots): 6,8=5 pips; 5,9=4; 4,10=3; 3,11=2; 2,12=1. Target 10+ pips across the 3 adjacent hexes. Resource priority: Ore/Wheat/Sheep for the city + dev card engine — highest tournament win rate. Wood/Brick opening is viable but demands aggressive expansion.
strategy_stealth: Target 10+ total pips on a spot

strategies.strategy_detailed: Fill resource gaps from your first pick. If you have production on a 6 or 8, use the second settlement to add a different resource type rather than doubling up. Port access is valuable here.
strategy_stealth: Grab a 2:1 port if resource allows

strategies.strategy_detailed: A 2:1 port adjacent to a high-probability hex of the matching resource is among the strongest setups. Ore 2:1 + high Ore production creates a near-unlimited Dev Card engine.
strategy_stealth: Match 2:1 port to your best hex

strategies.strategy_detailed: Use early Wood and Brick to secure a 3rd settlement site before opponents block key intersections. Expansion speed in turns 3-6 determines long-term territory control.

strategies.condition: Blocked from expansion by opponent roads
strategies.strategy_detailed: If routes are cut off, pivot to Dev Cards or trade aggressively for Ore/Wheat. City upgrades give you the same VP gain as a settlement without needing road access.

strategies.condition: Opponent requests a trade you don't need
strategies.strategy_detailed: Never give a resource that would help an opponent more than it helps you. Evaluate trades by what they build toward — if they're collecting Ore, don't give it.
As the leader, other players will trade against you. Rely on bank trades (port access) rather than player trades. Hoard Ore and Wheat to build Cities without needing help.
Actively trade to close the resource gap. Offer resources you have in excess and target the player most likely to trade out of self-interest. Trailing players should control pace of trades.
strategy_stealth: Trade with players near you in VP

strategies.strategy_detailed: Play Knights before opponents secure Largest Army (3 Knights). Largest Army is worth 2 VP and is often decisive. Move robber to your opponent's best hex to pressure their economy.
Play Road Building to reach Longest Road (worth 2 VP) or to cut off an opponent's expansion. Best used when you need 2 roads to claim the bonus or block a critical intersection.

strategies.condition: Have 3 Ore and 2 Wheat in hand
strategies.strategy_detailed: Build the city now. Cities give +1 VP plus double the settlement's production. Production scaling (not raw VP) is the real value — concentrated Ore/Wheat compounds. Banking risks robber loss and wastes the rare turn you actually have all 5 resources together.
strategy_stealth: Scaling > VP is real city value

strategies.strategy_detailed: Target the resource-richest hex of the player closest to winning. Steal their most important resource type. Never move robber to desert or low-probability hexes.
Look for a hidden VP card in your Dev Card pile. Hidden VPs (Chapel, Library etc.) let you win in one turn. Reach 8 VP and hold 2 VP cards to win on your next turn before opponents react.
strategy_stealth: Collect Dev Cards for hidden VPs
Strike when at 8 VP + 2 VP cards

strategies.condition: At 9 VP, one resource short of winning
strategies.strategy_detailed: At 9 VP, any trade or production that gives you the final resource wins the game. If opponents know you're at 9, no one will trade with you. Use Dev Card VP or bank trades to close without showing your hand.
strategy_stealth: Win via bank trade or Dev Card

strategies.strategy_detailed: Treat the leader at 9 VP as an existential threat. Refuse all trades, pile robber on their best hex, and coordinate with other players. Everyone at the table loses if they don't stop the leader.
strategy_stealth: Robber on their best hex every 7

strategies.strategy_detailed: Identify which VP bonuses are still unclaimed — Longest Road and Largest Army. Swapping one of these from an opponent is a 4 VP swing. Focus resources on whichever bonus is within reach.
strategy_stealth: 4 VP swing if you take + they lose
